title: Maine disables data breach notification portal after fake disclosures
description: Maine has taken its public data breach reporting portal offline after fraudulent breach disclosures were published on the state's website, prompting a review of procedures to prevent abuse in the future. [...]
author: Lawrence Abrams

title: phpBB forum fixes auth bypass bug lurking for a decade
description: A 10-year-old authentication bypass vulnerability discovered in the phpBB forum software allows an attacker to log in as any user, including administrators. [...]
author: Bill Toulas

title: Ukrainian national pleads guilty to role in Conti ransomware operation
description: A Ukrainian national extradited from Ireland to the United States last year has pleaded guilty to conspiracy charges tied to the Conti ransomware operation. [...]
author: Lawrence Abrams

title: Over 400 Arch Linux packages compromised to push rootkit, infostealer
description: More than 400 packages in the Arch User Repository (AUR) are distributing a Linux rootkit and infostealer malware targeting credentials and access tokens. [...]
author: Bill Toulas

title: Early Warning Signs of Supply-Chain Attacks Live in the Dark Web
description: GitHub access sales, leaked repositories, and stolen API keys can all become supply-chain attack footholds. Flare explores how underground forums expose early signals tied to software supply-chain risk. [...]
author: Sponsored by Flare

title: Microsoft fixes Windows update failures linked to WUSA installer
description: Microsoft has fixed a known issue that caused Windows updates released since May 2025 to fail when installed via the Windows Update Standalone Installer (WUSA) from a network share. [...]
author: Sergiu Gatlan

title: Pharma giant Novo Nordisk discloses breach of clinical trials data
description: Danish pharmaceutical giant Novo Nordisk, the world's largest producer of insulin, disclosed a data breach affecting patient information from some clinical trials. [...]
author: Sergiu Gatlan

title: CISA orders feds to patch actively exploited Ivanti flaw by Sunday
description: The U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) ordered government agencies to patch an actively exploited Ivanti Sentry flaw within three days, as mandated by the newly issued Binding Operational Directive (BOD) 26-04. [...]
author: Sergiu Gatlan
